title: rubus orthacanthus subsp. prasinus
intro: Rubus orthacanthus subsp. prasinus, also known as the Prasinus raspberry, is a subspecies of raspberry native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its greenish-yellow berries and fragrant flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus orthacanthus subsp. prasinus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to support overall health and well-being.
